stimulus:
The following text is from Zora Neale Hurston's 1948 novel Seraph on the Suwanee. Lila, a young girl, often visits the music room of a neighbor's house.

Lila thought that all the music in the world lived inside that piano bench and she had a plan about playing every piece in the world. She was working through the music a piece a day in the order the bench held it, and not skipping the dull ones. She remembered that the first piece had been an Andante. She had been playing one piece a day for many months now and she was still in the B pieces. Already she had practiced about Bourrees and Berceuses, Barcaroles and Bagatelles. For all her eagerness, she had to admit that some of the B pieces had been tough sledding. But Lila was a player.
stem: Which choice best states the main purpose of the text?

answerOptions.content: To describe a piece of music that Lila greatly admires
To explain why Lila prefers piano over other activities
To illustrate Lila's enjoyment of an unusual subject
To portray Lila's determination to meet a goal

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The text shows that Lila has set a goal of playing every piece in the music bench and is working through them one a day in order. Even as she progresses slowly (still in the B pieces after many months) and finds some pieces “tough sledding,” she does not give up because she is “a player.” The purpose is to portray her determination to reach a goal.

Choice A is incorrect because the text does not single out a piece she admires; she even works through dull ones. Choice B is incorrect because the text does not compare playing to other activities. Choice C is incorrect because the text does not present any of the pieces as unusual or as objects of special enjoyment.
